claims.text: The 1989 Hydro-Quebec solar storm knocked out power for 9 million people in Quebec for hours, demonstrating that even a moderate coronal mass ejection can collapse a regional power grid in minutes when it interacts with the Earth's protective magnetic field.

evidence.quote:
This episode of the W Files is brought
to you by PDS
Debt. In 1989, a solar storm hit Quebec.
The storm only lasted a few hours, but
it knocked out power for 9 million
people. And this was a blessing. Without
the Earth's magnetic field, the damage
would have been much
worse. Our magnetic field protects us.
It's been our shield for billions of
years, stopping deadly radiation and
keeping our planet survivable. But that
shield isn't perfect, and sometimes it
fails. Scientists recently found proof
inside an old stagmite from a cave in
China. Earth's magnetic field can flip,
not slowly over thousands of years,
fast, sometimes in just a hundred years.
The last big flip happened 780,000 years
ago, and we're overdue for a big one.
But smaller shifts, they happen much
more often. When the field fl

claims.rationale: The March 13, 1989 geomagnetic storm did cause a 9-hour blackout of Hydro-Quebec affecting about 6 million customers; '9 million people' is somewhat inflated but in the right ballpark since household impact extends past direct customer count. The framing as a CME-driven event is correct.

claims.text: Earth's magnetic field is real, weakening, and not behaving normally — strength has dropped about 10% since the 1800s, the South Atlantic anomaly is steadily growing, and the north magnetic pole is racing toward Siberia at roughly 34 miles per year.

shield weakens to about 10% strength.
When that happens, our defenses fail
quickly. Solar radiation hits the
surface. Plasma from the sun floods the
atmosphere. Our magnetic field is
weakening right now. It's already 10%
weaker since the 1800s. Over the South
Atlantic, satellites have been disabled.
And the North magnetic pole is moving 34
m a year towards Siberia and moving
faster every year. Something is
happening. Our ancestors have legends
about the last magnetic flip and danger
from the sun. The stories are
terrifying. They knew this was a cycle.
They left warnings for future
generations carved in stone. But those
warnings are being ignored. Maybe we
should start paying attenti

out.
The plasma apocalypse is something that
really scares me. But Thanosids,
zombies, ancient warnings carved in
stone. Let's separate facts from the
fiction. Earth's magnetic field is
weakening. The north magnetic pole is
shifting faster than before. That's
confirmed. Scientists measure it. Since
the 1800s, the field strength dropped
about 10%. The European Space Ay's Swarm
satellites track these changes. It's
real. The South Atlantic anomaly is
real. It fluctuates, but it's steadily
growing larger. It's not just causing
problems with satellites, either. It's
affecting X-ray equipment on the
surface. The big solar storms, those are
real, too. The Karantine event in 1859
happened. Telegraph systems failed.
Operators got electrocuted. Auroras were
seen near the equator. That's documented
history. The 1921 storm also real.
caused fires everywhere, disrupted
communication. Could a storm like
Carrington hit us today? Yes. Solar
scientists all agree. NASA, NOAA, FEMA,
they all know it's possible. A big
coronal mass ejection or

claims.rationale: All three sub-claims are well established: dipole moment has decreased ~9-10% over the past 170 years, the South Atlantic Anomaly is real and growing, and the north magnetic pole has been moving rapidly toward Siberia (recent rate up to 50-60 km/year — 34 mi/year ≈ 55 km/year is in range). ESA's Swarm constellation is the standard data source. The 'm a year' transcription is ambiguous (km vs mi) but the geophysics is solid.

claims.text: A Carrington-class solar storm hitting today's electrified, internet-dependent world would fry electronics and collapse modern infrastructure within minutes — power grids, communications, finance, and transportation could be unavailable for weeks to months across major cities.

Telegraph stations caught fire. Railroad

happened with our magnetic shield at
full strength. Imagine that same storm
hitting today with our weakened field.
The damage would be huge. Plasma could
fry every electronic device on Earth.
Phones, TVs, computers, the entire

off. Power grids would fail. Modern life
then breaks down in minutes. Think about
New York City, Chicago, Beijing in total
darkness for weeks. Chaos is an
understatement.
That was 1921. September 185

CME aimed at
Earth could knock out power grids for
weeks, maybe months. Our modern world
depends on electricity. Losing it would
be bad. Very bad. And this is a real
worry. But what about the other stuff?
Interdimensional beings, phantomazoids.
There's no scientific proof for any of
that. Conplan 8888, that's a real
military document, but it was a training
exercise. The military uses unrealistic
scenarios for training all the time so
they don't get mistaken for actual
plans. The document says it uses
fictional zombies as a teaching tool,
not because the Pentagon expects
zombies, allegedly, but it's a pretty
interesting document and really
detailed. It's kind of suspicious
actually. So, we're

claims.rationale: NASA, NOAA, and FEMA do all formally acknowledge a Carrington-scale CME as a catastrophic risk; National Academies and Lloyd's of London have produced impact studies estimating trillions in damage and multi-month recovery. 'Fries every electronic device' is overstated — battery-powered consumer devices and shielded electronics are largely safe; the existential risk is to high-voltage transformers and exposed long-conductor systems. So the magnitude framing is high but the directional risk is real.

claims.text: The September 1859 Carrington Event electrified telegraph wires worldwide, sparked fires in operator stations, sent compasses spinning, and pushed auroras far enough toward the equator to be seen in Cuba and read by from sky-light at 2 a.m. — proving solar weather can dramatically alter Earth's surface environment.

evidence.quote:
September 1859 was worse.
The Carrington event. Telegraph
operators got shocked from their own
machines. Sparks flew everywhere. Paper
caught fire. The northern lights moved
so far south they were seen in Cuba.
People woke up at 2 a.m. and birds
started singing. They thought it was
morning. The sky was bright enough to
read a newspaper outside. Frederick Drew
was in Kashmir, India. He wrote in his
journal, quote, "The whole northern
hemisphere was as light as though the
sun was beginning to rise." He described
the sky all crimson, a most vivid blood
red color. Drew wasn't a scientist. He
just wrote what he saw. Ships at sea
were lost. Compasses spun wildly,
useless. Sailors saw the ocean glow a
strange blue green light. This wasn't
sunlight. It wasn't the ocean. It was
plasma. Pure energy from space slipping
through our weak magnetic shield.
Scientists found proof of an even bigger
event. 1

claims.rationale: Every detail of the Carrington Event description is well documented: telegraph operator shocks and station fires (some operators continued working with batteries disconnected, powered by induced current alone), red-sky auroras seen as far south as the Caribbean, and the sky bright enough to read newspapers in the middle of the night. Frederick Drew's Kashmir journal entry is a real primary source.

claims.text: Cross-cultural winter solstice fire-keeping rituals — Yule logs, festival of lights, Egyptian demon days, Roman Saturnalia, Chinese Dongzhi, Persian Yalda — were not symbolic celebrations but coordinated ancient protection rituals against geomagnetic events, which ice cores show actually correlate in time with the festivals.

Christmas candle. These weren't just

protection
rituals. Ancient Egyptians celebrated
the demon days, a 5-day festival during
the darkest time of year. They believe
this is when the dark spirits could
enter our world. Their protection, keep
flames burning constantly. Roman
Saturnelia, Celtic winter solstice,
Chinese Dongji festival, all featured
continuous flames, light during the
darkest days. The Persian Shabialda
Festival celebrated light over darkness
by keeping candles burning all night
long. And here's what's interesting.
These rituals match periods of high
solar activity and weakened magnetic
fields. Icecore samples show proof of
major solar events that line up with the
timing of these ancient festivals. These
cultures had no contact if they all
share the same fear. Obsessed with
keeping flames burning. And at the exact
same time of year, they remembered
something we forgot, something ancient,
somet

going to do a whole
episode on that. Ancient solstice
festivals, they do share themes. Light,
fire, burning continuously, but experts
usually link this to seasons. Shorter
days, colder weather makes sense to
focus on fire and light. There's no
direct proof connecting these festivals
specifically to plasma events or
magnetic field shifts. It's an
interesting idea, but just an idea so
far. But this threat is real. a major
solar storm hitting a weakened magnetic
field that could cause blackouts and
communication failures for a long time.
We need to prepare and I'm worried we're
moving too slowly. We see ourselves as
masters of this planet. We split the
atom, mapped our DNA, connected every
corner of the world. Yet, one solar
hiccup could end it all. Our ancestors
understood their place in this cosmic
arrangement. They didn't try to conquer
nature. They respected its cycles, built
their lives around its rhythms, watched
for its warnings. We've lost that
humility. The ancients left us
instructions for survival. We would be
wise to learn from them. If we don't,
and a major plasma storm hi

claims.rationale: Winter solstice fire and light festivals are real and cross-cultural, but the standard anthropological explanation — they mark the astronomical reversal of the shortening days, when sunlight was psychologically and agriculturally needed most — is far more parsimonious than the proposed plasma-storm-protection thesis. Ice-core 10Be/14C spikes (Miyake events, etc.) do not align with most solstice festival origins, and the host explicitly walks this claim back in the verdict section.

claims.text: When Earth's magnetic shield collapses during a major solar event, plasma will reach the surface and may animate or possess organic matter — producing what governments call 'phantazoids' or zombies, beings from another dimension that look like microscopic life scaled to building size.

The DoD has con plan 888. It details

field collapses, they expect temporary

actually designed as a joke." Former

part of a classified exercise in 2009
cenamed Broken Mirror. It simulated a
massive solar flare triggering
electromagnetic anomalies worldwide.
Spark says his unit trained to contain
biological entities of unknown origin
entities appearing during the event. A
declassified CIA document from 1976 is
titled Elect

claims.rationale: There is no scientific basis for plasma animating organic matter or for interdimensional 'phantazoids'. Plasma is ionized gas; it cannot tunnel through the atmosphere intact to surface scale, and it has no documented interaction with consciousness or biology that would produce zombies. The host himself debunks this completely in the verdict section. This is pure speculative fiction presented as suppressed fact.

claims.rationale: CONPLAN 8888-11 is a real US Strategic Command document — its first line explicitly states it was created as a training tool for staff officers using zombies precisely because they're fictional, removing political sensitivities of using a real adversary. The 'Jim Spark / Broken Mirror' anecdote is unverifiable and likely fabricated for the video. The host's own verdict section concedes the document is real but a training exercise, not an actual plan.

claims.text: A declassified 1976 CIA report titled 'Electromagnetic Effects on Human Tissue' describes plasma exhibiting almost intelligent behavior and responding to consciousness, with three pages still classified — and Soviet researchers since the 1950s have called such entities 'phazoids' or 'plasma ghosts'.

evidence.quote:
Former
Marine Sergeant Jim Spark says he was
part of a classified exercise in 2009
cenamed Broken Mirror. It simulated a
massive solar flare triggering
electromagnetic anomalies worldwide.
Spark says his unit trained to contain
biological entities of unknown origin
entities appearing during the event. A

classified. The Russians studied this
since the 1950s. Their name for fazoids,

but only visible during geomagnetic
events. Many governments fear that if

millions of organic vessels capable of
using electricity all over the

claims.rationale: There is no public CIA report titled 'Electromagnetic Effects on Human Tissue' from 1976 with the described content (the host appears to be conflating CIA's STAR GATE-adjacent and Andrija Puharich-era documents into a fictional source). 'Phazoid' / 'plasma ghost' is a fringe term used by paranormal researchers, not a Soviet scientific category. The 'three pages still classified' rhetorical hook is a tell.

claims.text: Ancient megalithic structures (Nazca lines, Stonehenge, the pyramids) form a global planetary circuit deliberately engineered to channel plasma during geomagnetic events — they sit on conductive limestone bedrock, contain piezoelectric quartz, and their global positions create a vast electrical network.

evidence.quote:
ts knew about plasma.
They didn't just light candles. They
built structures. Massive structures to
channel the energy or maybe contain it.
The Nazcal lines in Peru. Massive
drawings on the ground. You can only see
them from high in the air. Some
scientists think these patterns might be
landing strips to attract plasma energy.
Channel it during geomagnetic events.
The desert floor there conducts
electricity. Well, an ideal material for
plasma interaction. Giant stone
structures like Stonehenge, the
pyramids, sites across South America.
They all share key electrical
properties. They sit on limestone
bedrock. Limestone conducts electricity.
Many of these structures contain quartz.
Quartz creates electrical charges under
pressure. Their positions around the
globe create a vast network, almost like
a planetary circuit. After the Apollo
missions ended, we pulled our space
programs back. So why

claims.rationale: This is a classic alternative-archaeology composite: real but minor properties (limestone has poor but nonzero conductivity, quartz is piezoelectric) are stitched into a sweeping coordinated-network claim with no engineering or archaeological basis. The Nazca lines aren't on limestone (they're on a desert pavement of red oxidized stones over light gypsum-rich subsoil), and there is zero evidence any of these monuments share a coordinated electrical purpose.

claims.text: Ancient Egyptian mummification was secretly an electrical engineering project: Cairo University researchers found that mummies contain conductive metals (copper, gold, special salts) arranged as deliberate circuit pathways through the body, designed to handle plasma energy.

evidence.quote:
world.
They're called human corpses. The
ancient Egyptians seem to understand
this energy. Researchers at Cairo
University found that ancient Egyptian
mummies have high levels of conductive
metals, copper, gold, special salts.
These materials form pathways through
the bodies. This wasn't just symbolism.
This was circuitry. So many governments
of the world, including our own, fear
that our weakening shield means the next
storm won't just turn off the lights. It
could turn on the
dead. The ancien

claims.rationale: Egyptian mummies do contain natron salts (sodium carbonate / sodium bicarbonate) used in desiccation, and decorative gold and amulets, but no Cairo University study has demonstrated any electrically functional 'circuitry' in mummified remains. The metals were used for symbolic and decorative reasons documented exhaustively in Egyptology, not as engineered current paths. This is fabrication built around real but misinterpreted material findings.

claims.text: Strange atmospheric sounds — sky trumpets and groaning sounds documented in over 40 countries — are warning signs of the plasma apocalypse, occurring most often in regions with unusual geomagnetic activity.

evidence.quote:
over 40 countries.
They happen most often in areas with

technology. That technology makes us

claims.rationale: Reports of unexplained 'sky sounds' / 'sky trumpets' do exist worldwide, but most documented examples have been traced to industrial sources, building/structure stress, atmospheric ducting of distant noises, or hoax videos. There is no peer-reviewed correlation with geomagnetic activity. The framing as a plasma-apocalypse warning is unsupported.

claims.rationale: This is the host explicitly separating the empirically grounded part of the story from the woo. The verdict accurately matches mainstream scientific consensus: solar-storm/grid risk is genuine and underaddressed, the paranormal embellishments are unsupported.

dependencies.rationale: Walks back the phantazoid/zombie speculation as having no scientific proof.
Walks back the solstice-as-plasma-defense thesis as 'just an idea so far'.
Confirms CONPLAN 8888 is a real document but a training exercise, not a real plan.
Reaffirms the Carrington-class storm risk to modern infrastructure.
